task_completion.description: Profile the runtime performance of CNN
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing the browser open to cnn.com with the DevTools Performance panel open and a completed performance recording (showing flame charts and summary metrics).

task_completion.description: Profile the runtime performance of Wikipedia
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing the browser open to wikipedia.org with the DevTools Performance panel open and a completed performance recording.

task_completion.description: Profile the runtime performance of GitHub
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing the browser open to github.com with the DevTools Performance panel open and a completed performance recording.

task_completion.description: Create the performance profile report file at the specified desktop path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, terminal, or file manager showing a file named 'performance_profile_report.txt' located at '/home/ga/Desktop/'.

task_completion.description: Extract and record timing values for Scripting, Rendering, and Painting for all three sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the report file. It must contain specific numerical timing values (likely in milliseconds) for 'Scripting', 'Rendering', and 'Painting' for CNN, Wikipedia, and GitHub.

task_completion.description: Include a summary identifying the site with the heaviest scripting load
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the report file for a concluding sentence or summary section that explicitly names which of the three websites had the highest/heaviest 'Scripting' time.

integrity.description: Agent actually used the browser's DevTools to profile the websites
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual Edge DevTools Performance panel actively recording or displaying a profile, rather than the agent just guessing or using a terminal script to fetch generic metrics.

integrity.description: Timing values in the report are derived from actual profiling
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers written in the report for Scripting, Rendering, and Painting should appear realistic (usually in milliseconds) and ideally match the summary pie chart seen in the DevTools screenshots.

integrity.description: The report was saved to the exact requested path
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the file path in the text editor or terminal is exactly '/home/ga/Desktop/performance_profile_report.txt' and not just printed to standard output or saved elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required report path is exactly /home/ga/Desktop/performance_profile_report.txt. The target domains to be profiled are cnn.com, wikipedia.org, and github.com. The DevTools Performance panel reports metrics for Scripting, Rendering, and Painting, typically using milliseconds (ms) as the unit. The agent must interact with the real websites and extract genuine performance data.
